name: Draft cited support replies from live public docs using You.com and Gmail

parameters.formDescription: Ask about a public, documented topic: a third-party API, a SaaS tool, a public standard, or a help or regulatory page. You.com researches live public sources and a cited draft reply is prepared in Gmail for a support agent to review and send.

values.placeholder: e.g., Does the Stripe API still support the legacy Charges endpoint, and what changed?
Optional: product or tool name, version, and what the customer already tried.

values.formSubmittedText: Thanks. You.com is researching public sources for this question now. A cited draft reply will be waiting in Gmail for a support agent to review and send. Nothing is emailed automatically. You can close this page.

nodes.name: When a Support Question Is Submitted

assignments.name: researchInput
errorMessage
assignments.value: ={{ $json.error?.message ?? 'The You.com research call failed. Check the You.com credential, plan limits, and the execution log.' }}

parameters.content:
## Draft cited support replies from live public docs using You.com and Gmail

### How it works

1. A support agent or customer submits a question about a public, documented topic through the form.
2. You.com researches live public sources in one call and returns an answer with inline citations and a source list.
3. A Code node formats the answer and its sources into an HTML email body.
4. Gmail saves the reply as a draft. It is never sent automatically.
5. Slack pings a support agent that a cited draft is ready to review and send.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Self-hosted n8n only: install the You.com community node `@youdotcom-oss/n8n-nodes-youdotcom` under Settings, Community Nodes.
- [ ] Add You.com and Gmail credentials, and a Slack credential if you want the ping.
- [ ] Open `Prepare Research Request` to set the research effort and the Slack member ID to mention.
- [ ] In `Notify Agent Draft Ready` pick the Slack channel for your support team.

### Customization

Scope the research prompt in `Prepare Research Request` to your own public docs, tune the research effort from lite to exhaustive, or swap the form trigger for a Gmail trigger on a support alias.

## Take in the support question
A form collects the question, optional context, and an optional customer email, then one Set node holds all the config and builds the research prompt.

## Research live public sources
You.com research runs one call over live public docs and standards and returns a cited answer. No private knowledge base, no RAG. A Code node turns the answer and its sources into an HTML email.

## Draft the reply, a human sends
Gmail saves the cited reply as a draft and never sends it. Slack tells a support agent it is ready to review and send.

## Handle a failed lookup
If the You.com call fails, the run builds a short failure notice and alerts the support team instead of failing silently.

## Self-hosted and human-in-the-loop
The You.com node is a community node and needs self-hosted n8n. The Gmail step only creates a draft. No path in this workflow sends an email on its own.
